title: hieracium macrostolonum subsp. cernuiforme
intro: Hieracium macrostolonum subsp. cernuiforme, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Hawkweed has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its potential to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Hawkweed is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is sometimes used in herbal medicine to promote wound healing and reduce the risk of infection.
